mission.title: Install and Configure Submariner on Kubernetes
mission.description: Submariner is a networking component designed to interconnect Pods and Services across multiple Kubernetes clusters, enabling seamless communication. Installing Submariner allows you to create secure and efficient networking between your clusters, regardless of the underlying network plugins.

steps.title: Add the Submariner Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Submariner Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add submariner https://submariner-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Submariner using Helm
steps.description:
Install Submariner with the specified version in a new namespace called 'submariner':
```bash
helm install submariner submariner/submariner --namespace submariner --create-namespace --version v0.22.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Submariner pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner
```

steps.title: Configure Submariner for your clusters
steps.description:
Run the Submariner configuration command to set up the necessary connections between your clusters:
```bash
subctl deploy-broker --kubeconfig=<path-to-your-kubeconfig>
```

steps.title: Validate Submariner is working
steps.description:
Validate that Submariner is functioning properly by checking the status:
```bash
subctl validate --kubeconfig=<path-to-your-kubeconfig>
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Submariner will show all pods in the 'submariner' namespace as running. Additionally, the validation command should confirm that the setup is correct and functioning as expected.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add submariner https://submariner-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install submariner submariner/submariner --namespace submariner --create-namespace --version v0.22.1

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Submariner Helm release from the 'submariner' namespace to remove all associated resources.
```bash
helm uninstall submariner --namespace submariner
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volumes that were created during the installation of Submariner.
```bash
kubectl delete crd submariners.submariner.io
kubectl delete crd gateways.submariner.io
kubectl delete crd connections.submariner.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace submariner
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'submariner' namespace and verify that it has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace submariner
kubectl get namespaces | grep submariner
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of Submariner by exporting the existing configurations and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all -n submariner -o yaml > submariner-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Submariner to the latest version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade submariner submariner/submariner --namespace submariner --version v0.22.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Submariner pods are running the new version and verify the upgrade was successful.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner
subctl validate --kubeconfig=<path-to-your-kubeconfig>
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If Submariner pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors and diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n submariner
```

troubleshooting.title: Submariner not connecting clusters
troubleshooting.description:
If Submariner is unable to connect clusters, verify the configuration and network settings. Check the status of the broker.
```bash
subctl validate --kubeconfig=<path-to-your-kubeconfig>
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version mismatch
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails due to version mismatch, ensure that the correct version is specified and that the Helm repository is updated.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade submariner submariner/submariner --namespace submariner --version v0.22.2
```

troubleshooting.title: Service discovery issues
troubleshooting.description:
If there are issues with service discovery across clusters, check the Submariner configuration and ensure that the required services are exposed correctly.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n submariner
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes version 1.24 or higher, and have both Helm and kubectl installed and configured.
